wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Veritas Shield project overview section focused on the contract analysis platform. Explain its purpose as an AI-powered legal document processing system, core architecture, and relationship to legal tech industry. Document the implementation approach using Django framework, key features including document management, text extraction, clause analysis, and knowledge graph integration. Include both conceptual overviews for legal professionals and technical details for developers. Use terminology consistent with legal tech and AI domains. Provide practical examples demonstrating contract analysis workflows. Document the system's position in the legal document automation space and competitive advantages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Veritas Shield introduction and purpose section focused on the AI-powered contract analysis platform. Explain the platform's mission to automate legal document processing and analysis, its position in the legal tech industry, and the problem it solves for law firms, corporate legal departments, and contract managers. Document the core value proposition of reducing manual contract review time by 80% through AI-powered text extraction, clause classification, conflict detection, and similarity analysis. Include market analysis showing the growing demand for contract automation solutions. Provide concrete examples of typical contract analysis workflows and their time savings. Address the competitive landscape and Veritas Shield's unique advantages in accuracy, scalability, and user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom User Model Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the custom User model implementation in Veritas Shield. Explain the extension of Django's AbstractBaseUser and PermissionsMixin classes, custom UserManager implementation with create_user and create_superuser methods, and field definitions including email, name, is_active, and is_staff. Document the USERNAME_FIELD configuration, REQUIRED_FIELDS setup, and object manager usage. Include practical examples of user creation, model instantiation, and integration with Django's authentication system. Address the advantages of email-based authentication over username-based systems, unique constraints, and model inheritance patterns used in the implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Overall System Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the overall system design of Veritas Shield. Explain the Django-based microservices architecture with clear separation of concerns across six specialized apps. Document the modular design philosophy, explaining how each app serves distinct business functions while maintaining loose coupling. Detail the technology stack choices including Django 6.0, PostgreSQL, Neo4j, and AI processing libraries. Explain the decision to use separate databases for relational data (PostgreSQL) and graph relationships (Neo4j). Include system boundaries, external dependencies, and integration points with AI processing services. Provide architectural diagrams showing the high-level component relationships and data flow patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: OCR Service Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the OCR service implementation using EasyOCR. Explain the OCRService class architecture, text extraction methodology, and confidence scoring mechanisms. Document the Reader initialization with English language support, text processing pipeline, and result formatting. Include practical examples of processing different image types, handling various text orientations, and optimizing OCR accuracy. Address configuration options for OCR parameters, language support expansion, and performance tuning strategies. Document common issues like poor image quality, text skewing, and mixed font types with their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Model and Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Document entity in Veritas Shield. Detail the Document model schema including all field definitions, data types, constraints, and relationships. Document the foreign key relationship with the User model, field validation rules, and default values. Explain the purpose and usage of each field including file, user, file_extension, uploaded_at, signed_at, lang, raw_text, confidence, and title. Include database schema diagrams showing entity relationships and indexes. Document the model's string representation method and its role in the document processing workflow. Address data validation, field constraints, and business rules enforced at the model level.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clause Extraction Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the clause extraction pipeline component. Explain the ClauseExtractor class implementation, text preprocessing techniques, pattern matching algorithms, and clause boundary detection. Document the integration with AI processing libraries, supported contract types, and extraction accuracy metrics. Include practical examples of clause extraction from various contract formats, handling of edge cases, and performance optimization strategies. Address configuration options for different contract domains and customization of extraction rules.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for document management endpoints. Document all HTTP methods for CRUD operations on documents including GET /documents/ for listing, POST /documents/ for creation, GET /documents/{id}/ for retrieval, PUT /documents/{id}/ for updates, and DELETE /documents/{id}/ for deletion. Include detailed request/response schemas for document upload, metadata handling, and file storage operations. Document the upload endpoint at /upload/ with multipart/form-data requirements. Provide practical examples showing document creation with file uploads, query parameters for filtering, and pagination responses. Include error handling for invalid file formats, size limits, and permission issues. Address file validation rules, supported formats, and storage location configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core data models in Veritas Shield. Document the Document model with its file handling, metadata fields, and AI processing attributes. Explain the User model customizations and authentication-related fields. Detail the Clause model structure for contract clause storage and analysis results. Document the AnalysisResult model for AI-generated insights. Include field definitions, data types, validation rules, and business constraints for each model. Provide examples of model instantiation and common query patterns. Address model inheritance, abstract base classes, and model mixins used throughout the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Containerization and Orchestration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive containerization documentation for Veritas Shield's database services. Detail the Docker Compose configuration for PostgreSQL and Neo4j containers, including service definitions, environment variables, volume mounts, and health checks. Explain container networking, port mappings, and persistence strategies. Document the Bitnami PostgreSQL container setup with security configurations, performance tuning parameters, and audit logging. Cover Neo4j graph database containerization with authentication, clustering options, and backup configurations. Include troubleshooting guides for common container issues, resource allocation, and scaling considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing Approach
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for unit testing approach in Veritas Shield. Document Django TestCase implementation patterns, model testing strategies for Document, User, and Clause models, and service layer testing for document processing and analysis services. Explain test isolation techniques, fixture usage for database testing, and mocking external dependencies. Include examples of testing custom user models, authentication logic, and business rule validation. Address testing Django REST Framework serializers, view logic, and URL routing. Document test organization patterns, naming conventions, and assertion strategies specific to contract analysis workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization and Scaling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive performance optimization documentation for Veritas Shield focusing on AI processing pipeline optimization, database query tuning, and system scaling strategies. Detail caching mechanisms for OCR results and processed documents, optimize Neo4j query performance through proper indexing and relationship patterns, and implement efficient batch processing for large document volumes. Document memory management techniques for OCR processing, PDF conversion optimization, and AI model inference acceleration. Include horizontal scaling approaches using load balancers and distributed processing patterns. Address performance monitoring with metrics collection, bottleneck identification, and capacity planning strategies for handling increased document throughput.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Token Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for JWT token management in Veritas Shield's authentication system. Document the token generation process, including access token and refresh token creation during user registration and login. Explain the CustomLoginView implementation using TokenObtainPairView and CustomTokenSerializer for token customization. Detail token validation, expiration handling, and automatic refresh mechanisms. Include practical examples of token extraction from requests, token storage recommendations, and token lifecycle management. Document the blacklist mechanism for logout functionality and token revocation. Address token security best practices, payload structure, and integration with frontend applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for Veritas Shield backend setup. Include prerequisites covering Python 3.8+, PostgreSQL, Neo4j, and Docker requirements. Document step-by-step installation process including environment setup, dependency installation, database configuration, and initial project setup. Provide clear instructions for running local development server, configuring environment variables, and accessing the API endpoints. Include troubleshooting common setup issues, verification steps to confirm successful installation, and next steps for development workflow. Address both Windows and Unix-like system setups with platform-specific considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features and Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Veritas Shield core features and capabilities section focused on the platform's AI-powered contract analysis functionality. Document the six main capabilities: automated document upload and storage, multi-format text extraction (PDF, images, Word), AI-powered clause extraction and classification, conflict detection between contract terms, similarity analysis across documents, and knowledge graph storage for semantic relationships. Explain each feature's technical implementation, user workflow, and business impact. Include concrete examples of how each capability improves contract review processes. Document the integration patterns between features and how they work together to provide comprehensive contract analysis. Address feature limitations and future enhancement plans.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for Veritas Shield's JWT-based authentication. Detail the authentication flow including login, logout, token refresh, and session management. Document JWT token generation, validation, and expiration handling. Explain the authentication views implementation, serializer configurations, and request/response handling. Include practical examples of authentication endpoints usage, token extraction from requests, and middleware integration. Address security considerations including token storage, CSRF protection, and authentication error handling. Document the relationship between authentication and user permissions, role-based access control, and integration with Django's permission system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the component architecture section. Thoroughly explain the implementation of key architectural patterns including Repository pattern for database access abstraction, Service Layer pattern for business logic encapsulation, and Factory pattern for document processor creation. Document the component relationships between DocumentService orchestration layer, OCRService and PDFService text extraction utilities, and AI analysis services. Explain how each component maintains single responsibility and communicates through well-defined interfaces. Include code examples showing pattern implementations, dependency injection strategies, and error handling approaches. Address component lifecycle management and service registration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: PDF Processing Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for PDF processing utilities using pdf2image. Explain the PDF-to-image conversion workflow, page handling strategies, and batch processing capabilities. Document the integration between pdf2image and OCR services, image quality optimization for scanned PDFs, and memory management during large document processing. Include practical examples of handling encrypted PDFs, password-protected documents, and mixed content PDFs. Address common challenges like resolution optimization, page range selection, and processing speed improvements. Document configuration options for DPI settings, color modes, and output formats.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Upload and Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the document upload and processing pipeline in Veritas Shield. Explain the complete workflow from file upload through text extraction to AI analysis completion. Document the DocumentService class and its methods for handling file uploads, format detection, and processing orchestration. Detail the file validation process, supported formats (PDF, images), size limitations, and error handling for corrupted files. Explain the integration with the text extraction engine and AI analysis services. Include practical examples of upload operations, processing status tracking, and error recovery mechanisms. Document concurrent processing capabilities, queue management, and performance optimization strategies for large document handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clause Classification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the clause classification system. Detail the ClauseClassifier implementation, machine learning models used for automatic category assignment, and classification accuracy metrics. Document the supported clause categories, confidence scoring mechanisms, and manual override capabilities. Include examples of classification results, model training requirements, and performance tuning guidelines. Explain the relationship between extracted clauses and classification outcomes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clause Analysis API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for clause analysis endpoints. Document the GET /files/documents/{doc_id}/clauses/ endpoint for retrieving extracted clauses with their analysis results. Include request parameters for clause filtering, sorting options, and pagination. Document response schemas containing clause text, classification categories, confidence scores, and analysis metadata. Provide examples of querying specific clause types, filtering by analysis confidence thresholds, and retrieving clause relationships. Include error responses for missing documents, processing failures, and invalid clause IDs. Address rate limiting for analysis requests and caching strategies for frequently accessed clause data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships and Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for entity relationships and database constraints in Veritas Shield. Detail foreign key relationships between Document and User models, and how clauses relate to documents. Explain many-to-many relationships, one-to-one associations, and cascading delete behaviors. Document database constraints including unique constraints, check constraints, and index optimizations. Include entity relationship diagrams showing table relationships, cardinality, and referential integrity rules. Explain the rationale behind relationship choices and their impact on query performance. Address constraint violations, cascade behaviors, and data consistency enforcement mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure and Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed infrastructure and environment configuration documentation for Veritas Shield deployment. Document Django settings configuration for different environments (development, staging, production), including database connections, cache settings, and security configurations. Explain ASGI and WSGI application setup for production servers like Gunicorn or uWSGI. Detail environment variable management, secret key handling, and configuration templates. Cover static file serving, media file storage options, and CDN integration. Include infrastructure requirements, hardware specifications, and dependency management. Address SSL/TLS configuration, reverse proxy setup, and load balancer configuration for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration and API Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive integration and API testing documentation for Veritas Shield's RESTful endpoints. Detail testing strategies for document management APIs, clause analysis endpoints, and authentication flows. Document request/response validation, status code testing, and data serialization verification. Explain testing of file upload workflows, OCR processing pipelines, and AI analysis results. Include examples of testing JWT authentication, permission-based access control, and error handling scenarios. Address testing of complex workflows involving multiple API calls, transaction handling, and cross-service dependencies. Document testing tools, fixtures, and test data management for realistic integration scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Hardening and Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed security hardening documentation for Veritas Shield covering input validation, XSS prevention, CSRF protection, and secure API design patterns. Document JWT token security implementation, authentication flow hardening, and authorization mechanisms. Detail secure file upload handling, content validation for different document formats, and protection against malicious document attacks. Include security headers configuration, CORS policy setup, and rate limiting implementation. Address secure database connections, environment variable management, and secrets handling. Document security testing approaches, vulnerability assessment procedures, and incident response protocols for security breaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Permission and Role System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the permission and role system in Veritas Shield. Detail the IsAuthenticated permission class implementation and its integration with Django REST Framework. Explain how authentication ties into Django's built-in permission system and user roles. Document the permission classes used across authentication endpoints and their security implications. Include examples of custom permission creation, role-based access control patterns, and integration with the user model. Address permission inheritance, object-level permissions, and best practices for securing API endpoints. Document the relationship between authentication tokens and permission validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for Veritas Shield system design. Describe the high-level Django-based microservices architecture, architectural patterns including Repository pattern, Service Layer pattern, and Factory pattern implementation. Document component interactions between document processing pipeline, AI analysis engine, and knowledge graph storage. Explain technical decisions around using PostgreSQL for relational data and Neo4j for semantic relationships. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing data flows from document upload through AI processing to analysis results. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, error handling, and performance optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack and Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Veritas Shield technology stack and dependencies section focused on the technical foundation of the contract analysis platform. Document the core technologies: Django 6.0 as the web framework, Django REST Framework for API development, PostgreSQL for relational data storage, Neo4j for graph database operations, and EasyOCR for optical character recognition. Explain the rationale behind each technology choice, integration patterns between components, and version compatibility requirements. Document external dependencies including ai_engine (custom AI processing library), pdf2image for PDF handling, and security libraries. Include infrastructure requirements, deployment considerations, and upgrade paths. Address performance characteristics and scalability implications of each technology choice.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Registration and Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user registration and profile management functionality. Document the user registration flow including form validation, password hashing, and account activation processes. Explain profile update operations, user data modification, and profile visibility controls. Include practical examples of registration API endpoints, profile management interfaces, and data validation rules. Address user data privacy considerations, profile picture handling, and integration with document access permissions. Document the relationship between user profiles and document ownership, audit trails for profile changes, and compliance with data protection regulations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow and Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for data flow architecture and processing pipeline. Document the complete data pathway from file upload through text extraction, AI analysis, and result storage. Explain the multi-stage processing pipeline including document ingestion, format detection, text extraction via OCR and PDF processing, AI-powered clause analysis, and knowledge graph integration. Detail the asynchronous processing patterns, batch operations, and error recovery mechanisms. Include sequence diagrams showing typical processing flows for different document types (PDF, images, text files). Document data transformation stages, validation checkpoints, and quality assurance measures throughout the pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Format Text Extraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for multi-format text extraction capabilities. Explain the format detection algorithms, automatic format identification, and unified extraction interface. Document support for various document formats including PDF, JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and DOCX. Include practical examples of format-specific processing pipelines, fallback mechanisms for unsupported formats, and quality assessment criteria. Address format-specific optimizations, compression handling, and metadata preservation. Document the extraction pipeline orchestration, error handling across different formats, and performance benchmarking results.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage and Media Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for document storage and media configuration in Veritas Shield. Explain the file storage backend setup, upload_to path configuration, and media URL handling. Document the storage location for contract files, file naming conventions, and directory structure organization. Detail the integration with Django's file handling system, including file validation, size limits, and format restrictions. Explain media configuration for serving uploaded files, static file handling, and production deployment considerations. Include examples of storage configuration for different environments (development, staging, production) and cloud storage integration options. Address security considerations for file access, temporary file handling, and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conflict Detection Algorithms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for conflict detection algorithms. Explain the ConflictDetector implementation, contradiction identification logic, and semantic analysis techniques. Document supported conflict types, detection confidence levels, and resolution recommendation systems. Include practical examples of conflict scenarios, false positive reduction strategies, and integration with similarity analysis. Address performance considerations for large-scale conflict detection across multiple documents.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document login, logout, and token management endpoints including request/response schemas for JWT authentication. Include detailed examples of login POST requests with username/password credentials and response containing access/refresh tokens. Document logout procedures, token refresh mechanisms, and session expiration handling. Provide security considerations for token storage, transmission, and rotation. Include error responses for invalid credentials, expired tokens, and account lockout scenarios. Address CORS configuration for frontend integration and mobile app authentication flows. Document password reset procedures and account verification processes if implemented.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration and Schema Evolution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for database migration management and schema evolution in Veritas Shield. Document the migration system setup, migration file structure, and version control approach. Explain initial schema creation through migrations, field additions, modifications, and deletions. Detail the migration execution process, dependency resolution, and rollback strategies. Include examples of common migration scenarios like adding new fields, creating relationships, and modifying constraints. Document best practices for safe schema changes, testing migrations, and handling production deployments. Address migration conflicts, data preservation during schema changes, and backward compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring and Maintenance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive monitoring and maintenance documentation for Veritas Shield production environment. Detail database monitoring setup for PostgreSQL and Neo4j, including performance metrics, query optimization, and capacity planning. Document log management strategies, centralized logging with ELK stack or similar solutions, and alerting configurations. Explain backup and recovery procedures for both relational and graph databases, including automated backup scheduling and restoration testing. Cover application monitoring with APM tools, error tracking, and performance profiling. Include maintenance schedules, database cleanup procedures, and security patch management. Address disaster recovery planning, high availability configurations, and business continuity procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI and OCR Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop specialized testing content for AI-dependent components and OCR services in Veritas Shield. Document testing strategies for EasyOCR integration, PDF processing workflows, and AI analysis pipelines. Explain mocking techniques for external AI services, OCR accuracy validation, and fallback mechanism testing. Include examples of testing document processing workflows, clause extraction validation, and similarity analysis algorithms. Address testing of machine learning model responses, confidence scoring, and edge case handling. Document testing approaches for different document types, image quality variations, and OCR failure scenarios. Explain performance testing for OCR processing, batch processing validation, and memory usage optimization during AI operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extensibility and Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create extensibility documentation for Veritas Shield detailing plugin architectures, custom document format processors, and AI model integration points. Document the factory pattern implementation for creating new document processors, extension points for adding custom clause analysis algorithms, and service layer customization mechanisms. Detail how to integrate new OCR engines, PDF processing libraries, and AI analysis models. Include guidelines for extending the knowledge graph schema, adding new document metadata fields, and implementing custom analysis workflows. Address backward compatibility considerations, version management for extensions, and testing strategies for custom implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Session Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for security and session handling in the authentication system. Document CSRF protection mechanisms, secure token transmission, and session management best practices. Explain error handling strategies for authentication failures, invalid tokens, and brute force protection. Detail security headers configuration, HTTPS enforcement, and token storage security considerations. Include practical examples of secure authentication flows, token refresh patterns, and logout procedures. Address common security vulnerabilities in authentication systems and their mitigation strategies. Document integration with Django's security middleware and recommended security configurations for production deployment.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive user management system documentation for Veritas Shield. Detail the custom User model implementation extending Django's AbstractUser, authentication configuration using JWT tokens, and permission system design. Document user registration flow, login/logout processes, password management, and profile operations. Include authentication endpoints specification, token lifecycle management, and security considerations. Explain the relationship between users and documents, access control mechanisms, and audit trail capabilities. Provide practical examples of user-related API usage, error handling strategies, and integration patterns with other system components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Business Use Cases and Benefits
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Veritas Shield business use cases and benefits section focused on the practical applications and ROI of the contract analysis platform. Document key use cases: due diligence analysis for M&A transactions, contract portfolio management for large corporations, legal team efficiency improvement, regulatory compliance monitoring, and contract risk assessment. For each use case, explain the specific problems solved, time and cost savings achieved, and measurable business outcomes. Include quantitative data showing reduction in contract review time, error rate improvements, and compliance benefits. Document different customer personas including law firms, corporate legal departments, and compliance teams. Address pricing models, implementation timelines, and success metrics. Provide case study examples with before/after scenarios and ROI calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and permissions documentation for the user management system. Detail password security policies, hash algorithms, and secure password handling practices. Document permission inheritance, group-based access control, and object-level permissions. Explain security middleware configuration, rate limiting, and brute force protection mechanisms. Include practical examples of permission checking, access control patterns, and security audit implementations. Address compliance requirements, data encryption at rest and in transit, and security monitoring capabilities. Document the integration with Django's built-in permission system and custom permission implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for integration patterns and external system connectivity. Document the integration with external AI processing services, cloud storage providers, and third-party authentication systems. Explain the plugin-based architecture for extending document format support and AI analysis capabilities. Detail the event-driven processing workflows, observer pattern implementations for notifications, and webhook integrations. Include examples of custom service registration, middleware integration patterns, and API gateway implementations. Address cross-cutting concerns like logging, monitoring, and distributed tracing across integrated systems. Document security considerations for external integrations and data privacy compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extraction Pipeline Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the text extraction pipeline architecture and workflow orchestration. Explain the end-to-end processing flow from document upload to extracted text output, including preprocessing, format detection, processing stage selection, and post-processing. Document the pipeline components, data transformation stages, and quality assurance checkpoints. Include practical examples of pipeline configuration, custom processing stages, and error recovery mechanisms. Address performance optimization strategies, parallel processing capabilities, and resource management. Document the integration with document services, error handling patterns, and monitoring capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Document API Endpoints and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for document management endpoints in Veritas Shield. Document all RESTful endpoints for document CRUD operations including upload, retrieve, update, and delete functionality. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Detail the document upload endpoint with file format validation, size limits, and processing status responses. Document the document retrieval endpoints for individual documents and document lists. Include request/response examples with JSON schemas, error codes, and validation messages. Explain pagination for document lists, filtering options, and search capabilities. Address rate limiting, security considerations, and CORS configuration for API access.

wiki_catalogs.name: Similarity Analysis Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the similarity analysis engine. Detail the SimilarityEngine implementation, embedding generation techniques, similarity calculation methods, and threshold configuration. Document supported similarity metrics, performance optimization for large document collections, and batch processing capabilities. Include examples of similarity search results, clustering algorithms, and integration with knowledge graph traversal. Address scalability considerations and memory optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for user management endpoints. Document user registration, profile management, and account settings endpoints. Include request/response schemas for user creation, profile updates, and account modification requests. Provide examples of user registration with email verification, profile update operations, and password change procedures. Document user permissions, role-based access control, and account status management. Include validation rules for user input, email format requirements, and password strength criteria. Address user data privacy considerations, GDPR compliance features, and data export capabilities. Document error responses for duplicate usernames/emails, validation failures, and permission denied scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Design Rationale
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation explaining the database design decisions and architectural choices in Veritas Shield. Detail why PostgreSQL was selected for relational data storage, including its advantages for document management, full-text search capabilities, and JSON field support. Explain the decision to use Neo4j for semantic relationships and knowledge graph storage, covering graph database benefits for contract analysis workflows. Address data consistency guarantees, ACID properties, and distributed transaction handling across different database systems.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing infrastructure documentation for Veritas Shield. Detail test database configuration, continuous integration setup, and automated testing workflows. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Veritas Shield target audience and positioning section focused on customer segmentation and market positioning strategy. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed text extraction engine documentation for Veritas Shield. Document configuration options for OCR parameters, language support, and performance tuning. wiki_catalogs.name: AI Analysis Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI analysis engine documentation for Veritas Shield. Explain the analysis workflow from raw text through structured clause extraction to actionable insights. Include practical examples of analysis results interpretation, confidence scoring, and result validation. Document the relationship between extracted clauses and knowledge graph storage, query optimization, and performance scaling considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Veritas Shield RESTful endpoints. Document all HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for document management, clause analysis, and authentication endpoints. Include detailed parameter specifications, response format examples, and error response codes. Provide practical curl examples and client implementation guidelines for each endpoint. Document rate limiting, pagination strategies, and API versioning approach. Include authentication flow examples, session management, and token refresh procedures. Address API security considerations, CORS configuration, and monitoring approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation for Veritas Shield. Detail entity relationship diagrams, field definitions, data types, and constraints for all models including Document, User, Clause, and AnalysisResult. Document primary/foreign key relationships, indexes, and performance optimizations. Explain data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity enforcement. Include database schema evolution through migration management, data lifecycle policies, and backup strategies. Document the rationale behind using PostgreSQL for relational data and Neo4j for semantic relationships. Address data security, access control, and compliance considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for Veritas Shield. Detail Docker containerization setup for PostgreSQL and Neo4j databases, environment variable configuration, and production deployment strategies. Document CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and deployment automation. Include infrastructure requirements, scaling considerations, and monitoring setup. Explain load balancing, reverse proxy configuration, and SSL certificate management. Address backup and disaster recovery procedures, log management, and performance monitoring. Provide troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and optimization strategies for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation for Veritas Shield. Detail unit testing approach for Django models and services, integration testing for API endpoints and business workflows, and mock service implementation for external dependencies. Document test data management, test database setup, and continuous integration testing. Include testing best practices for AI-dependent components, OCR service mocking, and database transaction handling. Explain test coverage requirements, performance testing strategies, and regression testing procedures. Address testing challenges specific to document processing, AI analysis, and multi-database architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create advanced topics documentation for Veritas Shield covering performance optimization, security hardening, scalability patterns, and extensibility mechanisms. Detail caching strategies, database optimization techniques, and AI processing performance tuning. Document security considerations including input validation, XSS prevention, CSRF protection, and secure API design. Explain horizontal scaling approaches, load distribution, and distributed processing patterns. Address extensibility points for adding new document formats, AI models, and analysis capabilities. Include troubleshooting guides for complex issues, debugging techniques, and system monitoring approaches for production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Veritas Shield Backend - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Veritas Shield is a sophisticated contract analysis platform designed to automate the extraction, classification, and analysis of legal contract clauses using advanced AI technologies. The system leverages machine learning algorithms and natural language processing to provide intelligent contract insights and risk assessment capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Automated Contract Processing**: Extract text from various document formats (PDF, images) using OCR technology
- **Intelligent Clause Classification**: Automatically categorize contract clauses using machine learning models
- **Knowledge Graph Integration**: Store and query contract information in a Neo4j graph database
- **Conflict Detection**: Identify potential contradictions and inconsistencies within contracts
- **Similarity Analysis**: Compare contracts to find similarities and patterns across documents

### Target Audience
- Legal professionals requiring contract analysis tools
- Compliance teams needing automated contract review
- Corporate legal departments managing large volumes of contracts
- Law firms seeking AI-powered contract processing solutions

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a modular Django architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Django Core] --> B[Apps Layer]
    B --> C[Authentication App]
    B --> D[Files App]
    B --> E[Analysis App]
    B --> F[Clauses App]
    B --> G[Text Extractor Engine]
    
    H[External Services] --> I[PostgreSQL DB]
    H --> J[Neo4j Graph DB]
    H --> K[OCR Services]
    
    L[AI Engine] --> M[Clause Extraction]
    L --> N[Clause Classification]
    L --> O[Conflict Detection]
    L --> P[Similarity Engine]
    
    D --> L
    E --> L
    F --> L
```

### Design Patterns
- **Repository Pattern**: Used for database operations and data access
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Encapsulates business logic in dedicated service classes
- **Factory Pattern**: Implemented for creating different types of document processors
- **Observer Pattern**: Applied in event-driven document processing workflows

### System Relationships
The architecture implements a layered approach with clear boundaries between presentation, business logic, and data access layers.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant API
    participant Service
    participant AI_Engine
    participant Database
    
    Client->>+API: Upload Document
    API->>+Service: Process Document
    Service->>+AI_Engine: Extract Text
    AI_Engine->>+Database: Store Results
    Database-->>-AI_Engine: Confirmation
    AI_Engine-->>-Service: Analysis Results
    Service-->>-API: Response
    API-->>-Client: Document Clauses
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Entry**: `manage.py` - Django command-line utility for administrative tasks
- **Configuration Hub**: `config/settings.py` - Centralized application configuration
- **URL Routing**: `config/urls.py` - Application URL patterns and routing configuration

### Core Modules
- **User Management**: `apps/users/models.py` - Custom user model with email-based authentication
- **Document Processing**: `apps/files/services/document_services.py` - Main document analysis service
- **Text Extraction**: `apps/text_extractor_engine/services/` - OCR and PDF processing utilities
- **API Endpoints**: `apps/files/views.py` - RESTful API endpoints for document operations

### Configuration Approach
The project uses Django's settings-based configuration with environment-specific overrides:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Settings Base] --> B[Development Settings]
    A --> C[Production Settings]
    A --> D[Environment Variables]
    
    E[Database Config] --> F[PostgreSQL]
    E --> G[Neo4j]
    
    H[Authentication] --> I[JWT Tokens]
    H --> J[OAuth2]
    
    I --> K[SimpleJWT]
    J --> L[Social Auth]
```

### External Dependencies
Key external libraries and services:
- **Django 6.0**: Web framework foundation
- **Django REST Framework**: API development framework
- **PostgreSQL**: Primary relational database
- **Neo4j**: Graph database for knowledge representation
- **EasyOCR**: Optical character recognition
- **pdf2image**: PDF to image conversion
- **ai_engine**: Custom AI processing library

### Integration Points
- **File Storage**: Local filesystem with media handling
- **Database Layer**: PostgreSQL for structured data, Neo4j for graph relationships
- **Authentication**: JWT tokens with SimpleJWT
- **External APIs**: Google OAuth2 for social authentication

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Document {
        +file: FileField
        +user: ForeignKey
        +file_extension: CharField
        +uploaded_at: DateTimeField
        +raw_text: TextField
        +confidence: FloatField
    }
    
    class User {
        +email: EmailField
        +name: CharField
        +is_active: BooleanField
        +is_staff: BooleanField
    }
    
    class DocumentService {
        +extractor: ClauseExtractor
        +classifier: ClauseClassifier
        +similarity_engine: SimilarityEngine
        +conflict_detector: ConflictDetector
        +db: Neo4jConnection
        +insert_document()
        +inspect_document()
        +get_document_clauses()
    }
    
    Document --> User : belongs to
    DocumentService --> Document : processes
    DocumentService --> ClauseExtractor : uses
    DocumentService --> ClauseClassifier : uses
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The platform provides comprehensive contract analysis capabilities through multiple integrated services:

1. **Multi-format Document Support**: Accepts PDF, JPG, PNG, and JPEG files
2. **Intelligent Text Extraction**: Combines OCR and direct text extraction
3. **Clause Classification**: Automatic categorization of contract terms
4. **Graph-based Knowledge Storage**: Neo4j integration for semantic relationships
5. **Conflict Detection**: Identifies contradictory clauses and inconsistencies
6. **Similarity Analysis**: Compares documents to find patterns and duplicates

### Implementation Highlights

#### Document Processing Pipeline
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> FileUpload
    FileUpload --> TextExtraction: PDF/Image Detected
    TextExtraction --> OCRProcessing: Image Content
    TextExtraction --> DirectExtraction: Text Content
    OCRProcessing --> ClauseExtraction
    DirectExtraction --> ClauseExtraction
    ClauseExtraction --> Classification
    Classification --> KnowledgeGraph: Store in Neo4j
    KnowledgeGraph --> AnalysisComplete
    AnalysisComplete --> [*]
```

#### AI Processing Architecture
The system integrates multiple AI services for comprehensive document analysis:

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Document Input] --> B[Text Extraction]
    B --> C[Clause Extraction]
    C --> D[Clause Classification]
    D --> E[Conflict Detection]
    E --> F[Similarity Analysis]
    F --> G[Knowledge Graph]
    
    H[OCR Engine] --> B
    I[PDF Parser] --> B
    J[ML Models] --> C
    K[Classification Models] --> D
    L[Detection Algorithms] --> E
    M[Embedding Models] --> F
```

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Upload] --> B[File Validation]
    B --> C[Format Detection]
    C --> D[Text Extraction]
    D --> E[Clause Processing]
    E --> F[AI Analysis]
    F --> G[Results Storage]
    G --> H[API Response]
    
    I[OCR Service] --> D
    J[PDF Service] --> D
    K[AI Engine] --> F
    L[Database] --> G
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
Veritas Shield demonstrates a well-structured Django application with clear modular separation and robust integration of AI services. The architecture successfully combines traditional web development practices with modern AI/ML capabilities, creating a comprehensive contract analysis platform.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Modular Design**: Clear separation of concerns across app boundaries
- **Scalable Infrastructure**: Supports both PostgreSQL and Neo4j for different data needs
- **AI Integration**: Seamless incorporation of multiple AI services
- **Extensible Framework**: Easy to add new document types and analysis capabilities

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Error Handling**: Implement comprehensive error handling and logging throughout the AI pipeline
- **Performance Optimization**: Consider caching strategies for frequently accessed contract data
- **Security Enhancements**: Implement additional security measures for document processing
- **Testing Coverage**: Expand unit and integration testing for AI components

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Add resilience to external AI service dependencies
2. **Add Monitoring**: Integrate monitoring and alerting for AI processing failures
3. **Optimize Database Queries**: Implement pagination and indexing for large document collections
4. **Enhance Security**: Add file validation and virus scanning capabilities
5. **Documentation**: Create comprehensive API documentation and developer guides

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Microservice Migration**: Consider breaking down monolithic AI services into independent microservices
- **Cloud Deployment**: Implement container orchestration with Kubernetes
- **Real-time Processing**: Add WebSocket support for real-time analysis updates
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement machine learning model training and evaluation pipelines

---

**Sources:**
- [manage.py](manage.py)
- [settings.py](config/settings.py)
- [urls.py](config/urls.py)
- [models.py](apps/users/models.py)
- [document_models.py](apps/files/models.py)
- [document_services.py](apps/files/services/document_services.py)
- [views.py](apps/files/views.py)
- [serializers.py](apps/files/serializers.py)
- [pdf_service.py](apps/text_extractor_engine/services/pdf_service.py)
- [ocr_service.py](apps/text_extractor_engine/services/ocr_service.py)
- [postgresql_docker_compose.yaml](docker_files/postgresql_docker_compose.yaml)

</blog>
